Omalotheca nanchuanensis is classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida, the subclass Asteridae, and the order Asterales. intro: Omalotheca nanchuanensis, also known as Nanchuan cottonweed, is a perennial plant species in the family Compositae, native to China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis, wounds, and even certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Omalotheca nanchuanensis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Omalotheca nanchuanensis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and salves for topical application.
